summary: Decomposed the bizPA MVP PRD into granular backend, UI, governance, sync, export, and workflow validation tasks aligned to the MVP build order and quarter-ready trust goals.
warnings: The PRD does not define an existing code architecture, so task outputs assume new modules, schemas, services, and UI surfaces will need to be introduced conservatively.

Biometric confirmation is specified as an MVP requirement, but the implementation mechanism is not defined; tasks assume a pluggable biometric/auth confirmation adapter rather than platform-specific hardware support.
Cloud sync, notification delivery, and connector work are scoped to MVP-safe foundations and export compatibility rather than full enterprise collaboration or direct filing integrations.
UI tasks include inferred local viewability requirements per the provided UI delivery contract, including start scripts, localhost URLs, smoke startup validation, and screenshot evidence.

tasks.title: Define Canonical Entity And Event Schemas
tasks.workstream_goal: Establish immutable, export-friendly schemas for all MVP business records and event history.
tasks.purpose: Create the canonical schema set for monetary entities, non-monetary entities, snapshots, and immutable business events so all later services share one source of truth.
tasks.input: PRD sections covering structured data model, inbox history, immutability rules, snapshot contents, and export requirements.
tasks.output: Versioned schema definitions, field dictionary, validation rules, and quarter reference conventions used by capture, readiness, snapshot, and export flows.
tasks.action: Design and document the canonical storage and validation contracts for every MVP entity and immutable event type, including required vs optional fields and quarter labeling rules.
verification: Schema covers all MVP monetary and non-monetary entity types from the PRD.
Validation rules reject missing required monetary fields and unsupported state values.
Field dictionary maps cleanly to export package requirements and snapshot contents.

tasks.title: Implement Monetary Integrity Rules And State Machine
tasks.workstream_goal: Establish immutable, export-friendly schemas for all MVP business records and event history.
tasks.purpose: Enforce no-deletion, no silent mutation, and valid lifecycle transitions for committed monetary records.
tasks.input: Canonical schemas from A1 and PRD sections defining lifecycle discipline, correction rules, and trust principles.
tasks.output: Domain service or policy module enforcing valid transitions, void/replace flows, correction metadata, and immutable commit protections.
tasks.action: Implement a central rules engine that prevents in-place post-commit amount edits, blocks deletions of committed monetary items, and requires correction via explicit replacement or state-change events.
verification: Committed monetary amounts cannot be changed in place through supported service paths.
Invalid lifecycle transitions are rejected with explainable errors.
Void, replace, and supersede flows create traceable correction metadata.

tasks.title: Build Immutable Business Event Log
tasks.workstream_goal: Establish immutable, export-friendly schemas for all MVP business records and event history.
tasks.purpose: Persist an append-only event history for all business actions that drive the inbox, audit trail, readiness changes, and governance tracking.
tasks.input: Schemas from A1 and integrity rules from A2.
tasks.output: Append-only event store model, event writer service, event type catalog, and retrieval API for chronological views.
tasks.action: Create the event logging infrastructure used whenever an entity is created, corrected, status-changed, snapshotted, closed, reopened, or auto-commit settings change.
verification: Each supported business action appends a new event without mutating prior event rows.
Event retrieval returns newest-first chronological business history without technical noise.
Audit payload includes actor, timestamp, linked entity, and event metadata.

tasks.title: Implement VAT And Quarter Classification Service
tasks.workstream_goal: Establish immutable, export-friendly schemas for all MVP business records and event history.
tasks.purpose: Standardize VAT calculations and quarter assignment so readiness, exports, and snapshots remain deterministic.
tasks.input: Entity schemas from A1 plus PRD VAT and quarter behavior requirements.
tasks.output: Reusable service for net/VAT/gross validation, VAT type classification, and quarter reference derivation.
tasks.action: Implement deterministic helpers that validate amount consistency, classify input/output VAT, and assign records to the correct quarter based on transaction date and policy.
verification: Service derives consistent VAT totals for supported monetary item types.
Quarter assignment is deterministic for sample dates across quarter boundaries.
Invalid amount combinations are rejected before commit.

tasks.title: Build Capture Composition And Preview Pipeline
tasks.workstream_goal: Deliver low-friction capture flows that structure activity before trusted commit.
tasks.purpose: Support the required monetary capture flow of Capture -> Parse -> Preview -> Confirm -> Commit with editable composition state.
tasks.input: Schemas and services from A1 to A4 plus PRD capture and preview requirements.
tasks.output: Composition-state model, parser output contract, preview payload builder, and commit orchestration layer.
tasks.action: Implement the non-committed composition state and preview generation path for all monetary captures, separating editable draft data from committed financial truth.
verification: Monetary captures remain outside the inbox and readiness calculations until confirmed.
Preview payload contains all required review fields and confidence indicators.
Confirm action commits the entity, logs an event, and triggers downstream recalculations.

tasks.title: Implement Voice Parsing For Core Monetary And Note Intents
tasks.workstream_goal: Deliver low-friction capture flows that structure activity before trusted commit.
tasks.purpose: Translate voice-first user input into structured candidate entities for invoices, receipts, quotes, payments, bookings, and notes.
tasks.input: Capture pipeline from B1 and PRD sample utterances and supported inputs.
tasks.output: Intent parser, extraction rules, fallback handling, confidence scoring, and normalized composition payloads.
tasks.action: Implement voice parsing for the MVP utterance set, including confidence scoring and graceful fallback to manual correction when extraction is uncertain.
verification: Sample utterances for invoice, receipt, quote, payment, and note map to the correct entity type.
Low-confidence parses are marked for explicit review before commit.
Parser output conforms to the composition payload contract used by B1.

tasks.title: Create Monetary Capture UI With Preview And Confirm
tasks.workstream_goal: Deliver low-friction capture flows that structure activity before trusted commit.
tasks.purpose: Provide the primary operator UI for voice/manual capture, preview review, edit, confirm, and cancel actions for monetary entries.
tasks.input: Composition pipeline from B1, voice parser from B2, and canonical field definitions from A1.
tasks.output: User-facing capture screen or panel, preview modal/state, edit controls, and commit-trigger wiring.
tasks.action: Implement the main UI surface for capture so a user can submit voice or manual monetary input, review the structured preview, edit fields, and confirm or cancel safely.
verification: A start script is present that launches the UI locally and prints the access URL.
The task documents the localhost URL to open for the capture workflow.
Running the start script completes a startup smoke test without immediate crash.
A screenshot is captured showing the working capture preview UI in the verification folder.
Confirm, Edit, and Cancel controls are visible and wired to the expected draft behavior.

tasks.title: Implement Non-Monetary Auto-Commit Capture Flow
tasks.workstream_goal: Deliver low-friction capture flows that structure activity before trusted commit.
tasks.purpose: Support low-friction commit for notes, reminders, attachments, and non-valued bookings without forcing preview confirmation.
tasks.input: Capture contracts from B1 and non-monetary scope from the PRD.
tasks.output: Simplified capture handlers, auto-commit rules for non-monetary items, and event logging integration.
tasks.action: Implement direct capture-and-commit behavior for non-monetary item types while preserving event history and excluding unsupported monetary shortcuts.
verification: Supported non-monetary inputs commit without the monetary preview step.
Committed non-monetary items appear in business history where relevant.
Non-monetary auto-commit does not bypass monetary integrity rules.

tasks.title: Build Business Activity Inbox Query And Filter Layer
tasks.workstream_goal: Turn immutable business events into clear operator workflows and entity drill-downs.
tasks.purpose: Provide the retrieval, filtering, and presentation contract for the chronological inbox that excludes technical/system noise.
tasks.input: Event log from A3 and display/filter requirements from the PRD.
tasks.output: Inbox query service, filter model, event-to-display mapping, and pagination or list retrieval API.
tasks.action: Implement inbox retrieval that returns newest-first business events with filter support for All, Needs Review, Financial, Quotes, Payments, and Alerts.
verification: Inbox results are strictly chronological and exclude technical sync or system log noise.
All required filter modes return only the expected subsets of events.
Display payload includes all required badges and linked entity metadata.

tasks.title: Create Business Activity Inbox UI
tasks.workstream_goal: Turn immutable business events into clear operator workflows and entity drill-downs.
tasks.purpose: Deliver the main history view where users review business activity, filter items, and open related entities.
tasks.input: Inbox service from C1 and canonical event display fields from A3.
tasks.output: User-facing inbox screen with chronological list, filter controls, badges, and item navigation.
tasks.action: Implement the inbox UI so users can scan business events, filter the stream, and tap through to deep-dive entity views.
verification: A start script is present that launches the inbox UI locally and prints the access URL.
The task documents the localhost URL to open for the inbox screen.
Running the start script completes a startup smoke test without immediate crash.
A screenshot is captured showing the inbox list with filters and badges in the verification folder.
Opening an inbox item navigates to its linked entity view.

tasks.title: Implement Atomic Inbox Actions And Event Emission
tasks.workstream_goal: Turn immutable business events into clear operator workflows and entity drill-downs.
tasks.purpose: Enable the allowed single-item actions from the inbox while preserving immutable history and correction discipline.
tasks.input: Inbox services from C1, integrity rules from A2, and event log from A3.
tasks.output: Action handlers for mark paid, update due date, add missing category, add missing counterparty, and convert quote to invoice.
tasks.action: Implement supported inbox actions so each edit updates the underlying entity through approved domain rules and emits a new business event.
verification: Each supported action updates the target entity through a valid service path.
Every inbox action appends a new event visible in the inbox history.
Unsupported bulk edits and forbidden monetary rewrites are rejected.

tasks.title: Create Deep Dive Entity Views For Invoices Receipts Quotes Payments And Notes
tasks.workstream_goal: Turn immutable business events into clear operator workflows and entity drill-downs.
tasks.purpose: Provide full-context views for one entity at a time without mixing unrelated business history.
tasks.input: Canonical schemas from A1, event log from A3, and inbox navigation from C2.
tasks.output: Entity-specific UI views and retrieval endpoints covering header data, attachments, notes, status, and entity timelines.
tasks.action: Implement deep-dive pages or panels for the main entity types, showing full context and correction state while keeping the scope entity-specific.
verification: A start script is present that launches the entity view UI locally and prints the access URL.
The task documents the localhost URL to open for an entity deep-dive route.
Running the start script completes a startup smoke test without immediate crash.
A screenshot is captured showing at least one working invoice or receipt deep-dive view in the verification folder.
Deep-dive timelines show only events related to the selected entity.

tasks.title: Implement Readiness Rules Engine And Weighted Scoring
tasks.workstream_goal: Continuously calculate an explainable quarter-readiness score for the active quarter only.
tasks.purpose: Compute the deterministic readiness percentage and issue weighting for active-quarter tax readiness.
tasks.input: Canonical schemas from A1, VAT service from A4, integrity rules from A2, and PRD weighting tiers.
tasks.output: Rules engine for issue detection, weighted score calculation, severity assignment, and explanation payloads.
tasks.action: Implement the active-quarter readiness engine that evaluates critical monetary integrity, structural completeness, and optional quality issues with deterministic scoring.
verification: Score calculation is deterministic for identical quarter data inputs.
Tier 1 monetary integrity issues reduce the score more heavily than Tier 2 and Tier 3 issues.
Stored snapshot scores are not recalculated when live quarter data changes later.

tasks.title: Build Readiness Drill Down And Resolution Linking
tasks.workstream_goal: Continuously calculate an explainable quarter-readiness score for the active quarter only.
tasks.purpose: Expose the missing-percentage breakdown so users can inspect issues and navigate directly to the relevant fix flow.
tasks.input: Readiness engine from D1 and entity/inbox routes from C2 to C4.
tasks.output: Issue breakdown service, actionable issue list, and linking from each issue to the related record or correction flow.
tasks.action: Implement the issue drill-down layer that groups active-quarter readiness problems and links each item to the exact record or workflow needed to resolve it.
verification: Every readiness issue includes an explanation and a valid navigation target.
Drill-down only reports on the active quarter and excludes stored historical snapshots.
Resolving a sample issue updates the readiness breakdown on recalculation.

tasks.title: Create Tax Readiness UI
tasks.workstream_goal: Continuously calculate an explainable quarter-readiness score for the active quarter only.
tasks.purpose: Provide a visible readiness dashboard for the current quarter, including percentage, issue count, severity, and drill-down actions.
tasks.input: Readiness services from D1 and D2.
tasks.output: User-facing tax readiness screen with score summary, issue list, drill-down navigation, and active-quarter context.
tasks.action: Implement the tax readiness UI so a user can see how quarter-ready they are, understand what is missing, and jump directly into corrective actions.
verification: A start script is present that launches the readiness UI locally and prints the access URL.
The task documents the localhost URL to open for the readiness dashboard.
Running the start script completes a startup smoke test without immediate crash.
A screenshot is captured showing the readiness percentage and issue drill-down in the verification folder.
The UI clearly distinguishes current active-quarter readiness from historical snapshot data.

tasks.title: Implement Snapshot Creation And Immutable Storage
tasks.workstream_goal: Create immutable, versioned quarter snapshots with controlled close and reopen behavior.
tasks.purpose: Generate a frozen quarter snapshot package with metadata, totals, readiness score, warnings, and file references.
tasks.input: Schemas from A1, event history from A3, readiness score from D1, and export requirements from the PRD.
tasks.output: Snapshot service, immutable snapshot records, version numbering logic, and stored snapshot metadata.
tasks.action: Implement snapshot generation that freezes the quarter state at a point in time, stores all required metadata, and preserves the snapshot as immutable after creation.
verification: At least one snapshot can be created even when unresolved issues exist, with warnings attached.
Generated snapshots store all required metadata, included transactions, totals, and readiness score.
Previously created snapshots remain unchanged after later live-quarter edits.

tasks.title: Build Post Snapshot Diff And Reversioning Logic
tasks.workstream_goal: Create immutable, versioned quarter snapshots with controlled close and reopen behavior.
tasks.purpose: Detect quarter changes after snapshot creation, block redundant versions, and generate diffs before new versions are created.
tasks.input: Snapshot store from E1, event log from A3, and PRD diff requirements.
tasks.output: Change-detection service, diff payloads, changed-since-last-snapshot flagging, and no-change guardrails.
tasks.action: Implement the logic that compares current quarter state against the most recent snapshot, exposes the delta, and allows new snapshot versions only when meaningful changes exist.
verification: System flags the quarter when live data changes after a snapshot exists.
Diff output lists additions, voids, adjustments, revenue impact, and VAT impact before creating a new version.
Attempting to create a new version with no changes is rejected with a clear explanation.

tasks.title: Implement Quarter Close And Reopen Governance Flow
tasks.workstream_goal: Create immutable, versioned quarter snapshots with controlled close and reopen behavior.
tasks.purpose: Support optional manual close, explicit reopen with confirmation and reason, and quarter-state enforcement.
tasks.input: Snapshot service from E1, event log from A3, and governance requirements from the PRD.
tasks.output: Quarter lifecycle service, close/reopen policy checks, reason capture, and blocked-entry enforcement for closed quarters.
tasks.action: Implement manual close and reopen flows that timestamp state changes, block new monetary entries while closed, and log reopen reason plus confirmation metadata immutably.
verification: Closed quarters reject new monetary entries and blocked edits that would affect the quarter.
Reopen requires explicit confirmation metadata and a stored reason.
Close and reopen actions append immutable events visible to audit and business history consumers.

tasks.title: Create Snapshot And Quarter Lifecycle UI
tasks.workstream_goal: Create immutable, versioned quarter snapshots with controlled close and reopen behavior.
tasks.purpose: Provide the operator screens for generating snapshots, reviewing diffs, re-downloading versions, and closing or reopening quarters.
tasks.input: Snapshot services from E1 to E3 and readiness outputs from D3.
tasks.output: User-facing snapshot management UI with version list, diff review, integrity warnings, and quarter-state controls.
tasks.action: Implement the snapshot and lifecycle UI so a user can create a snapshot, inspect changes since the prior version, re-download earlier versions, and manage quarter close/reopen actions.
verification: A start script is present that launches the snapshot UI locally and prints the access URL.
The task documents the localhost URL to open for snapshot management.
Running the start script completes a startup smoke test without immediate crash.
A screenshot is captured showing the snapshot list or diff review screen in the verification folder.
The UI prevents redundant version creation when no changes exist and surfaces integrity warnings clearly.

tasks.title: Implement Accountant Ready Export Package Builder
tasks.workstream_goal: Produce one-click quarter export packages that accountants can use without cleanup.
tasks.purpose: Generate the full export package for a snapshot, including CSV, summary document, integrity report, and metadata bundle.
tasks.input: Snapshot records from E1 and canonical schemas from A1.
tasks.output: Export builder producing structured CSV, VAT summary PDF or equivalent, integrity report, snapshot metadata, and packaged download artifact.
tasks.action: Implement package generation from immutable snapshot data so each export is reproducible and ready to share without spreadsheet cleanup.
verification: Export package includes all mandatory files for a selected snapshot version.
Structured CSV columns align with the canonical schema and accountant-ready formatting goal.
Re-exporting the same snapshot produces equivalent contents and metadata.

tasks.title: Add Export Compatibility Mappings And Validation
tasks.workstream_goal: Produce one-click quarter export packages that accountants can use without cleanup.
tasks.purpose: Prepare export mappings for generic accountant-ready output plus future Xero and QuickBooks-compatible structures without overbuilding direct integrations.
tasks.input: Export builder from F1 and integration direction from the PRD.
tasks.output: Mapping definitions, export validators, and fixture-based compatibility checks for generic and connector-oriented formats.
tasks.action: Define and validate export mappings that preserve clean internal structure while making downstream accountant or connector ingestion straightforward.
verification: Generic accountant-ready export passes validation against the canonical field set.
Connector-oriented mapping definitions exist for Xero-compatible and QuickBooks-compatible layouts.
Validation reports clearly identify missing or incompatible fields before packaging.

tasks.title: Implement Local First Sync Queue And Delta Sync Service
tasks.workstream_goal: Provide resilient local-first continuity, backup, and operational alerts without corrupting financial truth.
tasks.purpose: Enable background sync of local changes to cloud storage with offline queueing, delta transfer, tenant isolation, and transport-safe foundations.
tasks.input: Canonical schemas from A1, event log from A3, and PRD sync model.
tasks.output: Sync queue, delta computation service, retry handling, tenant-aware sync envelope, and conflict policy hooks.
tasks.action: Implement a light MVP sync subsystem that queues local changes, syncs deltas in the background, tolerates offline operation, and preserves financial immutability constraints during replay.
verification: Local changes are queued and retried when simulated connectivity is unavailable.
Delta sync transmits only changed records or events rather than full state each time.
Sync logic does not overwrite immutable committed monetary truth silently.

tasks.title: Add Sync Conflict Guardrails And Health Telemetry
tasks.workstream_goal: Provide resilient local-first continuity, backup, and operational alerts without corrupting financial truth.
tasks.purpose: Handle acceptable last-write-wins cases while preventing silent corruption of immutable financial records and exposing sync health signals.
tasks.input: Sync service from G1 and integrity rules from A2.
tasks.output: Conflict resolution policies, sync health metrics, backlog reporting, and error telemetry surfaces for governance dashboards.
tasks.action: Implement conflict handling and telemetry so operational status is visible and financially sensitive conflicts are surfaced rather than silently merged.
verification: Acceptable non-financial conflicts follow the defined resolution policy.
Financially sensitive conflicts generate explicit warnings or blocked resolutions instead of silent overwrite.
Telemetry exposes queue backlog, error rate, and last successful sync details.

tasks.title: Implement Notification Engine For Critical Important And Informational Events
tasks.workstream_goal: Provide resilient local-first continuity, backup, and operational alerts without corrupting financial truth.
tasks.purpose: Surface relevant operational alerts and opportunities without spam, prioritizing quarter readiness and business follow-up value.
tasks.input: Event log from A3, readiness outputs from D1 and D2, and sync health from G2.
tasks.output: Notification rule engine, severity model, delivery queue, and in-app notification payloads.
tasks.action: Implement a notification engine that creates critical, important, and informational alerts for overdue invoices, readiness changes, stale snapshots, deadlines, and meaningful operational signals.
verification: Critical, important, and informational categories are generated according to the PRD examples.
Notifications include actionable links and avoid duplicate spam for the same unresolved condition.
Delivery or display status is traceable for audit and system health monitoring.

tasks.title: Create Homepage Command Centre UI
tasks.workstream_goal: Deliver an energetic business cockpit that answers what needs attention today.
tasks.purpose: Replace a passive homepage with a business command centre showing momentum, attention items, performance grid, insight feed, and central capture action.
tasks.input: Readiness data from D1, notifications from G3, core entity metrics from A1 and C1, and PRD homepage requirements.
tasks.output: User-facing homepage dashboard with live momentum bar, attention panel, compact performance grid, smart insight feed, and central capture CTA.
tasks.action: Implement the MVP homepage as a clear operator dashboard focused on current business attention and fast entry into the capture workflow.
verification: A start script is present that launches the homepage UI locally and prints the access URL.
The task documents the localhost URL to open for the command centre.
Running the start script completes a startup smoke test without immediate crash.
A screenshot is captured showing the homepage command centre in the verification folder.
The homepage clearly answers what needs attention today and includes a central capture action.

tasks.title: Implement Role Policy And Governance Configuration Model
tasks.workstream_goal: Provide secure administrative control, governed auto-commit, and clear operator visibility for risky actions.
tasks.purpose: Define the owner, admin, staff, and future accountant-read-only access model plus tenant-level policy settings for VAT, numbering, readiness, thresholds, features, and auto-commit caps.
tasks.input: PRD governance and control centre sections.
tasks.output: RBAC model, policy configuration schema, audit hooks, and authorization checks for admin-only actions.
tasks.action: Implement the governance configuration backbone that secures administrative features and stores tenant policy settings used across the platform.
verification: Role checks distinguish owner, admin, staff, and future read-only capabilities as specified.
Policy changes are stored with audit metadata and actor attribution.
Protected administrative actions cannot be executed by unauthorized roles.

tasks.title: Build Governed Auto Commit Policy And Safety Override Engine
tasks.workstream_goal: Provide secure administrative control, governed auto-commit, and clear operator visibility for risky actions.
tasks.purpose: Allow time-bounded monetary auto-commit only under owner policy, user opt-in, confirmation, risk acceptance, and override rules.
tasks.input: Governance model from I1, capture pipeline from B1, and PRD auto-commit rules.
tasks.output: Auto-commit policy service, duration handling, eligibility checks, threshold overrides, expiry handling, and event logging integration.
tasks.action: Implement governed auto-commit so eligible users can enable daily, weekly, or monthly modes under policy caps while forcing confirmation for high-risk or low-confidence captures.
verification: Auto-commit cannot be enabled when owner policy disallows it or requested duration exceeds the cap.
Low-confidence or over-threshold monetary captures bypass auto-commit and require confirmation.
Enable, disable, expiry, and policy-affecting events are logged immutably.

tasks.title: Create Control Centre And Auto Commit Visibility UI
tasks.workstream_goal: Provide secure administrative control, governed auto-commit, and clear operator visibility for risky actions.
tasks.purpose: Deliver the administrative control centre and visible app state changes required for governance, policy management, sync health, and auto-commit awareness.
tasks.input: Governance services from I1, auto-commit engine from I2, and sync telemetry from G2.
tasks.output: User-facing control centre screens, policy controls, system health panels, session visibility, and persistent auto-commit banners or badges.
tasks.action: Implement the control centre UI for admins and the persistent operator-facing indicators that make auto-commit impossible to miss across the app.
verification: A start script is present that launches the control centre UI locally and prints the access URL.
The task documents the localhost URL to open for the governance or control centre screen.
Running the start script completes a startup smoke test without immediate crash.
A screenshot is captured showing the control centre and visible auto-commit state in the verification folder.
Auto-commit state remains visually obvious during capture-related workflows.

tasks.title: Establish Integration Boundary Contracts And Plugin Registry
tasks.workstream_goal: Prepare clean extension points for future connectors without overbuilding direct integrations in the MVP.
tasks.purpose: Create the base plugin and connector contract for future payments, communications, calendar, and export-oriented accounting integrations.
tasks.input: PRD integration categories and governance requirements from I1.
tasks.output: Plugin registry model, connector interface contracts, enable/disable controls, version tracking, and staged rollout metadata.
tasks.action: Implement the minimal registry and interface contracts needed to track, enable, and safely evolve future integrations without coupling the MVP core to specific vendors.
verification: Plugin registry supports enable, disable, version tracking, and tenant scoping.
Connector contracts are documented for accounting export, payments, communications, and calendar categories.
Registry state changes are auditable and governable through the admin model.

tasks.title: Build End To End Workflow Test Suite For MVP Critical Paths
tasks.workstream_goal: Prove the MVP critical paths are reliable, explainable, and safe under realistic workflow sequences.
tasks.purpose: Automate the highest-value workflows so refactors do not break capture, readiness, snapshotting, exports, or governed controls.
tasks.input: All completed domain services and UI entry points across workstreams A through I.
tasks.output: Automated integration and lifecycle tests covering voice invoice, receipt, snapshot, post-snapshot change, close/reopen, auto-commit, and export flows.
tasks.action: Create an end-to-end test suite with fixtures and mocks that validates the MVP priority workflows and failure modes defined in the PRD.
verification: Voice invoice workflow validates parse, preview, confirm, event log, readiness update, and sync trigger behavior.
Snapshot and post-snapshot change workflows validate immutable versions, diff generation, and re-download behavior.
Manual close/reopen and auto-commit workflows validate governance rules, logging, and blocked unsafe actions.

tasks.title: Add Reliability Explainability And Regression Harness
tasks.workstream_goal: Prove the MVP critical paths are reliable, explainable, and safe under realistic workflow sequences.
tasks.purpose: Cover non-functional requirements for trust, explainability, safety, and auditability with deterministic regression checks.
tasks.input: Critical domain and UI services from all prior workstreams.
tasks.output: Regression harness, golden fixtures for readiness and exports, and failure-mode tests for integrity, sync, and notifications.
tasks.action: Implement regression tests and fixtures that prove readiness explanations, snapshot diffs, export contents, and immutable history remain stable across changes.
verification: Readiness explanations and diff outputs remain stable against golden fixtures for representative quarter data.
Regression tests catch silent mutation, silent deletion, and silent sync corruption scenarios.
Audit traces can be reconstructed for tested workflows from emitted events and stored snapshots.
